steps.label: cause
steps.text: Healthy ripe grapes are dried after harvest before alcoholic fermentation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Water evaporates from the berries, concentrating sugars, acids, flavour compounds and phenolics before the must is fermented.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The resulting wine can have high potential alcohol, concentrated ripe or dried-fruit flavours and substantial body and structure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: partial grape drying concentrates the must and explains Amarone's powerful, full-bodied style.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
